cover.title: AI ships code by copy-paste
cover.sub: GitClear counted 211 million changed lines. As AI spread, pasted code overtook refactored code for the first time.

slides.title: copy-paste rose 8.3% to 12.3%
slides.body: Share of changed lines that were pasted, 2020 to 2024. Roughly a 48% jump as AI tools spread across teams.

slides.title: refactoring fell 24.1% to 9.5%
slides.body: Moved (refactored) lines collapsed. In 2024, pasted code passed refactored code for the first time GitClear has measured.

slides.title: churn climbed 3.1% to 5.7%
slides.body: Code rewritten within two weeks of commit. The early-warning sign of debt AI defers onto your team, later.

slides.title: speed now, maintenance bill later
slides.body: The speed is real. So is the debt. GitClear's method runs on your own repo, so you can measure your duplication trend.

cta.title: measure your duplication trend
cta.sub: A straight read of your agent setup in your real repo, sources and method included. The prioritized plan is yours to keep.
